text: You shout 'HELLO' into the dark. The echo answers 'HELLO,' then, after a pause, 'WHO TOLD YOU THAT NAME?'

success.text: Through careful questioning you determine the echo is guarding a stash it can't spend, being an echo. It trades coordinates for conversation.

fail.text: The echo answers every question with your own question, but smugger. You leave with a headache and existential doubts.

outcomes.text: 'THANK YOU,' says the echo, mollified. It guides you around a pit as a courtesy. Manners: still undefeated.
The echo repeats it to something bigger, deeper in the cave. You hear footsteps. You are suddenly very fast.
The echo laughs. An ancient tension breaks. It shares the cave gossip, including where the previous adventurer dropped their purse.

text: Something enormous is asleep across the passage. Its breathing sounds like a forge. A sign around its neck: 'DO NOT WAKE. SERIOUSLY. -MANAGEMENT.'

success.text: You ghost past it, stepping between snores. On the far side, someone has left a tip jar labeled 'FOR THE QUIET ONES.' You take a cut.

fail.text: You step on the one crunchy rock in the cave. The something opens one eye, decides you're not worth digesting, and swats you into a wall.

outcomes.text: Management was right. Management is always right. You survive on pure cardio.
It purrs. It PURRS. You have adopted something the size of a barn, emotionally if not logistically. It gifts you a shiny thing from its hoard.
You live to be a coward another day. The something snores approvingly.

text: The ceiling is a galaxy of glow worms, blue-green constellations rearranging slowly. It is genuinely, unfairly beautiful for a hole in the ground.

choices.text: Harvest a few for the glow

success.text: You bottle a soft constellation. Alchemists pay well for portable starlight.

fail.text: The worms coordinate. Every light goes out at once, and in the black something drips meaningfully. You leave with wet boots and an apology.

outcomes.text: You lie on a flat rock and watch the ceiling drift. Whatever was wound tight in you unwinds a notch. The worms neither know nor care. Perfect.
It tastes like mint and poor judgment. Your tongue glows for a day. Taverns will love you tonight.
Science reports: no. Your stomach glows faintly, visibly, through you. Alarming for everyone.

text: An underground lake, black and still as held breath. Something in the middle of it is faintly, patiently, ringing. Like a bell. Like an invitation.

success.text: The bell is a sunken shrine's. Around its clapper, offerings of centuries: coins, rings, one impressively preserved cheese. You tithe yourself a share.

fail.text: The cold gets a vote and vetoes you halfway. You crawl out shivering. The ringing sounds distinctly amused now.

outcomes.text: The fish are pale, eyeless, and delicious-looking in a way you choose to trust. Provisions acquired.
You catch a boot. Inside the boot: coins. Inside the coins: no further mysteries, thankfully. Just coins.

choices.text: Skip a stone at the ringing

outcomes.text: Four skips, then silence. Total silence. The ringing does not resume. You feel you have won something and also ended something. The cave feels emptier on the way out.

text: A chamber of crystals, each humming a different note. Together they almost make a chord. Almost. One crystal is flat, and the whole cave seems embarrassed about it.

success.text: A quarter-turn, a held breath, and the chord LOCKS. The harmonic wave that rolls through the cave shakes loose centuries of dropped coins from the ceiling cracks.

fail.text: You make it worse. The dissonance is now weaponized. Every crystal turns its hum on you until you leave, ears ringing with disapproval.

outcomes.text: You supply the missing note yourself. For one shining minute, you are part of a geological choir. Nothing about your life feels small right now.
You are also flat. The cave now has two problems. The crystals dim pointedly.

choices.text: Chip off a crystal to sell

outcomes.text: The note dies in your hand; loose crystals don't sing. It's still pretty, and pretty sells, but you'll remember the silence at odd moments for years.
